name: Moncayo Natural Park Loop from Beratón
name_original: Alto de los Almudejos, Nacimiento del río Isuela y pueblos de la comarca del Moncayo – circular desde Beratón
description: This 26.9-mile moderate mountain biking route from Beratón explores Alto de los Almudejos and the Isuela River source in Moncayo Natural Par
region_intro: Mountain biking around Beratón offers access to the striking natural landscapes of the Moncayo Massif. The region is characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and rocky outcrops, providing a dynamic environment for riders. This topography creates varied terrain, from smooth singletracks to technical descents, making it a notable destination for MTB routes Beratón. Best mountain bike trails around Beratón The most popular mountain bike trail is Moncayo Loop, a 30.3 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 37 minutes to complete, offering extensive views of the Moncayo Massif. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Moncayo Summit Loop, a difficult 23.4 miles (37.6 km) path that includes significant elevation gain towards the summit. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro Gordo MTB Route, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Beratón is defined by the Moncayo Massif, rolling hills, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike trails Beratón. The routes in Beratón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 4 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Beratón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Beratón?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails available around Beratón, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels within the Moncayo Massif region.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Beratón?
A: The mountain bike trails around Beratón primarily range from moderate to difficult. There are 7 moderate routes and 13 difficult routes, providing challenging options for experienced riders. The terrain often features rolling hills, dense forests, and rocky outcrops.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Beratón?
A: Currently, there are no easy-rated mountain bike trails listed directly from Beratón. The available routes are categorized as moderate or difficult, suggesting they are better suited for riders with some experience or families with older, more experienced children.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on mountain bike trails near Beratón?
A: The trails around Beratón vary significantly in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985076453/moncayo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 30.3 miles (48.7 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986809904/calcena-purujosa-trasobares-calcena-circular-por-la-cara-oculta-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena - Purujosa - Trasobares - Calcena&lt;/a&gt; route extends to about 34.0 miles (54.8 km).

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Beratón?
A: Yes, many routes in the Beratón area are circular, designed to begin and end in the same location, allowing you to explore the stunning natural scenery of the Moncayo Massif. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986801726/moncayo-summit-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails around Beratón?
A: The trails offer extensive views of the Moncayo Massif and take you through diverse natural scenery. You might encounter natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Beratón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the striking natural landscapes of the Moncayo Massif, the varied terrain from smooth singletracks to technical descents, and the challenging nature of the routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Beratón?
A: Beratón's location near the Moncayo Massif means conditions can vary. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, while winters might bring snow and colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, and you might also come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Snow Well of Prado de Santa Lucía&lt;/a&gt;, a historical man-made monument. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia&lt;/a&gt; is also a nearby settlement of interest.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in the Beratón area?
A: Yes, there are 7 moderate mountain bike trails around Beratón. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986742842/cerro-gordo-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gordo MTB Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.0 miles (29.0 km) long and leads through varied terrain, typically completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike routes near Beratón?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986762639/vistas-a-la-muela-de-horcajuelo-cerro-morron-y-las-penas-de-herrera-circular-desde-anon-de-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vistas a La Muela de Horcajuelo, Cerro Morrón y las Peñas de Herrera&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and technical sections. This particular route is about 26.5 miles (42.6 km) with over 1400 meters of ascent.
tips: Dirt road located in the Sierra del Moncayo that offers us a magnificent panoramic view of the surrounding area, near the town of Beratón. A dream place for lovers of mountain biking or gravel.

At this point, we come across the source of the Isuela River, surrounded by pure nature in the Sierra del Moncayo. In the place, we will find an information panel that will give us more details about the river.
Nice path under the shade of the trees not far from the town of Cueva de Ágreda.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it offers a rewarding experience of the Moncayo's natural beauty, it's best suited for cyclists with some experience, as mountain biking routes in the Moncayo often include forest tracks, natural paths, and potentially some technical sections.

faqs.question: Is this a circular mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that starts and ends in the village of Beratón, allowing you to return to your starting point without needing additional transport.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Beratón serves as the departure point for many cycling routes, so you should find suitable parking within or near the village to begin your journey.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the Alto de los Almudejos mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery typical of the Moncayo region. You'll encounter highlights such as a Panoramic View of Sierra del Moncayo Near Beratón, the Source of the Isuela River, and a scenic Forest Path Near Cueva de Ágreda. The Moncayo area is known for its striking contrast of ecosystems, from rocky areas and oak groves to beech forests.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the diverse ecosystems of the Moncayo region, spring and autumn are generally ideal. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the potential snow and ice of winter at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of Etapa 2: Santuario del Moncayo - Purujosa, [CIMA ZA02] Beratón, Camino Viejo de Aragón, cordel de Ganados, Camino de Borobia, camino del Molino Nuevo, Camino del Moncayo, Camino de Los Esquiñones, and camino de Valdehierro.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Moncayo Natural Park generally allows dogs. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and enjoyment of all trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for mountain biking in the Moncayo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for general mountain biking on established trails within the Moncayo Natural Park. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation in Beratón?
faqs.answer: Beratón is a rural village and a common starting point for routes in the area. While specific details vary, you can generally expect basic amenities, including some accommodation options, and potentially a local bar or cafe to refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Moncayo region offers varied terrain. You can expect to ride on a mix of forest tracks and natural paths. While the route is rated moderate, be prepared for some sections that might be more technical, typical of mountain biking in natural park environments.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to Beratón?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Beratón, a small rural village, may be limited. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or consider private transport as the most reliable way to reach the trailhead.
